coverLines: TRUMP'S NEWS CIRCUS: NOW WITH EXTRA FLAMES!
OVER $1 BILLION IN GRAFT — YOUR TAX DOLLARS AT WORK!
ATTORNEY GENERAL: TRUMP FAMILY IRS AUDIT-PROOF FOR ETERNITY!

pages.title: FROM THE EDITOR'S BUNKER
pages.salutation: Dear Reader Who Immediately Asked For A Helmet,
paragraphs: We had a plan to pace ourselves this issue. We were going to cover one thing at a time. We made a list. The list caught fire.

In the past 36 hours: Trump juggled an Iran war, a Cuba indictment, an IRS deal, and midterm primaries while standing on a Coast Guard ship. The Attorney General quietly made the Trump family IRS audit-proof for eternity — a phrase we had to read three times to confirm was real. Over $1 billion in graft is being redirected to allies, January 6th rioters, and cop killers, a use of public funds our accountants describe as "extremely creative."

Meanwhile: Xi and Putin toasted pipelines and AI chips in Beijing. Kentucky ejected Thomas Massie via the Endorsement Machine. Raúl Castro, 94, received a federal indictment rolled like a cigar. The Israeli Knesset is fizzing toward a snap election. The WHO reports 600 suspected Ebola cases and 139 suspected deaths in Congo and Uganda, and unlike everything else in this issue, that one is not funny at all — the health workers racing to contain it are heroes.

On a lighter note: Nashville got the 2030 Super Bowl. The Wall Street bull slipped on an oil slick. And we added a classified ads section this issue because the jobs being advertised are too real to ignore.
We asked for a helmet. They sent us a subscription form.
pages.ps: P.S. — The IRS audit-proof thing is real. We checked. We are very tired.

pages.title: INSANE MAGAZINE MASTHEAD

pages.scene: Trump caricature as a circus ringmaster in red coat and top hat, standing on a tiny Coast Guard ship podium, juggling four large flaming balls labeled IRAN WAR, CUBA INDICTMENT, IRS DEAL, and MIDTERM PRIMARIES. His expression is gleeful and completely unworried. Behind him: a cheering crowd of supporters in MAGA hats, a grinning GOP elephant driving a steamroller labeled ENDORSEMENT MACHINE, and a Kentucky ballot box launching a suited incumbent (Massie) through the air like a jack-in-the-box. Flames everywhere. Confetti. Mad Magazine caricature style.
pages.eyebrow: COVER STORY · THE NEWS CIRCUS · COAST GUARD EDITION
pages.heading: TRUMP'S NEWS CIRCUS!
pages.subheading: NOW WITH EXTRA FLAMES — IRAN, CUBA, IRS, AND PRIMARIES, ALL JUGGLED SIMULTANEOUSLY
pages.deck: Trump Speaks At Coast Guard Academy Amid Iran War; Endorsement Machine Ejects Massie In Kentucky; Cuba Indicted; IRS Dealt — Ringmaster Has Not Dropped A Single Ball, Technically
pages.caption: ★ "This is fine," said the ringmaster, as the tent burned. ★
paragraphs: President Trump delivered the commencement address at the United States Coast Guard Academy this week, speaking to graduating cadets about service, sacrifice, and leadership while simultaneously: managing an active Iran war, processing a Cuba indictment, overseeing an IRS restructuring deal, and monitoring midterm primary results — a feat his aides described as "focused multitasking" and everyone else is calling "a juggling act with flaming objects."
The juggling act is, on technical review, going. None of the flaming balls have landed on anyone important. The tent is on fire in several places. The ringmaster appears to be enjoying himself.

The Endorsement Machine — the GOP elephant-driven steamroller that has been flattening insufficiently loyal Republicans for two years — claimed its latest casualty in Kentucky, where Thomas Massie lost his primary to Trump-backed Ed Gallrein. Massie, who had served eight terms and was known primarily for showing up to votes, showed up to this one and lost it. The machine is reportedly already fueled and looking at the next map.

"This is a great day for America," Trump told the graduating cadets. "We have the best Coast Guard. The most beautiful ships. You're going to do things nobody has ever done before." He did not specify what things. The cadets applauded. The ships floated. The balls stayed in the air.
For now.
pages.pullQuote: "Focused multitasking." — Trump aide, describing the simultaneous juggling of four international crises

pages.scene: A giant money cannon labeled YOUR TAX DOLLARS AT WORK fires bags of cash in three directions simultaneously. First stream: to suited allies in MAGA hats catching money in briefcases. Second stream: to masked January 6 rioters in KILL COPS shirts with their arms open. Third stream: to a recipient labeled COP KILLERS holding a sign that says THANK YOU. In the center a smiling Trump caricature pulls the cannon's lanyard with one hand and holds a sign reading AMERICA FIRST (with asterisk). An accountant in the background faints over a ledger. A small sign reads DEMOCRACY SOLD SEPARATELY. Mad Magazine style with maximum visual irony.
pages.eyebrow: FINANCE · YOUR TAX DOLLARS AT WORK*
pages.heading: OVER $1 BILLION IN GRAFT!
pages.subheading: SPOILS FOR ALLIES, JAN 6 RIOTERS, AND COP KILLERS — *DEMOCRACY SOLD SEPARATELY
pages.deck: Trump Administration Redirects Over $1 Billion In Public Funds To Political Allies, January 6th Participants, And Individuals Convicted Of Killing Police Officers — Accountants Request Reassignment
pages.caption: ★ The asterisk on AMERICA FIRST is doing a lot of work. ★
paragraphs: The Trump administration has directed over $1 billion in public funds toward a distribution list that includes political allies, participants in the January 6th Capitol assault, and individuals convicted of killing police officers — a spending allocation that budget analysts describe as "creative," opposition lawmakers describe as "corruption," and the White House describes as "taking care of the people who matter."

The recipients — categorized by INSANE's finance desk as "Allies," "Rioters," and "Cop Killers" in descending order of optics — represent what administration sources call a "loyalty dividend" and what the rest of the English language calls something else.

January 6th participants who assaulted police officers on the Capitol steps will receive funds from the same public treasury that employs those officers. Several recipients currently carry convictions for violence against law enforcement. The accounting team that processed the allocations has, per sources, requested reassignment to the puzzle page.

A sign in the relevant White House office, photographed by a staff member and shared widely before being taken down, read: "YOUR TAX DOLLARS AT WORK." Below it, in smaller print: "*Democracy sold separately."
"We're taking care of our people," a senior official said. Our people was not defined. The billion dollars was.
pages.pullQuote: "We're taking care of our people." — Senior official, regarding $1B+ distributed to allies, rioters, and cop killers

pages.scene: The Attorney General caricature in a judge's robe uses an enormous golden rubber stamp reading AUDIT-PROOF FOREVER on a stack of Trump family tax files. The stamp is so large it takes both hands. The IRS building in the background has its sign changed to TRUMP FAMILY EXEMPTION OFFICE. A line of regular Americans stretches around the block outside holding their own audit notices. One sign in the line reads I AM NOT A TRUMP. Inside the office a single IRS agent sits at a desk with a sign that says EVERYONE ELSE: STILL AUDITABLE. A small pile of tax files for the Trump family is labeled CLOSED FOREVER. Mad Magazine caricature style, maximum bureaucratic irony.
pages.eyebrow: TAX LAW · THE GIFT THAT KEEPS NOT GIVING
pages.heading: AUDIT-PROOF FOR ETERNITY!
pages.subheading: ATTORNEY GENERAL MAKES TRUMP FAMILY IRS AUDIT-PROOF — EVERYONE ELSE: STILL VERY AUDITABLE
pages.deck: In A Move Tax Law Experts Describe As 'Extraordinary,' The Attorney General Has Shielded The Trump Family From IRS Audits Indefinitely — The Rest Of America's Tax Files Remain Open For Business
pages.caption: ★ "CLOSED FOREVER" — the most efficient tax policy in American history. ★
paragraphs: The Attorney General has taken action to make the Trump family IRS audit-proof for eternity, a phrase that tax law experts describe as "extraordinary," "without modern precedent," and "the kind of thing that usually only happens in countries we write editorials about."

The protection — which applies to the President and his family members indefinitely, with no sunset clause, no review mechanism, and no defined scope — was implemented quietly, which tax attorneys note is "the traditional method for implementing things that would be loudly objected to if implemented loudly."

Outside the IRS building, which sources say has informally retitled its front office the Trump Family Exemption Office, a line of regular Americans clutching audit notices stretches around the block. A handwritten sign at the front of the line reads I AM NOT A TRUMP. The sign has not helped.

One IRS agent, reached by INSANE at his desk inside the building, confirmed that the Trump family files have been marked CLOSED FOREVER in the system. "The rest of you are still very auditable," he added, in what sources describe as "a tone of resigned professionalism."

The AG's office said the action was "appropriate and lawful." Tax law experts said they would need more time to review that characterization. They have a lot of time. The audit protection does not expire.
pages.pullQuote: "The rest of you are still very auditable." — IRS agent, regarding everyone who is not the Trump family

entries.def: An IRS examination of tax records. Available to: everyone except the Trump family.
The duration of the Trump family audit exemption. No sunset clause.
The AG's characterization. Under review by everyone else.

pages.scene: A Kentucky GOP primary ballot box is built like a giant jack-in-the-box toy. The lid has just sprung open, launching Thomas Massie caricature high into the air in a suit and tie, briefcase flying, papers scattering. The crank of the jack-in-the-box is labeled TRUMP ENDORSEMENT. A grinning GOP elephant drives a steamroller labeled ENDORSEMENT MACHINE through the background. Ed Gallrein caricature stands next to a WINNER ribbon. In the foreground, a scoreboard reads LOYALTY: REQUIRED / EIGHT TERMS: INSUFFICIENT. Mad Magazine caricature style, spring-loaded energy.
pages.heading: KENTUCKY FRIED INCUMBENT!
pages.subheading: MASSIE LOSES PRIMARY TO TRUMP-BACKED GALLREIN — EIGHT TERMS NOT ENOUGH; LOYALTY SCORE INSUFFICIENT
pages.deck: Rep. Thomas Massie, Eight-Term Kentucky Republican, Loses GOP Primary To Trump-Backed Ed Gallrein — The Endorsement Machine Claims Another One — Dunk Tank Industry Reports Record Quarter Again
pages.caption: ★ Eight terms. One endorsement against him. Spring-loaded. ★
paragraphs: Rep. Thomas Massie of Kentucky, who has represented his district for eight terms and was known principally for libertarian-leaning votes and occasionally showing up to things, showed up to his own primary this week and lost it — a result that political analysts are calling "the Endorsement Machine at full operating capacity."

Massie's offense, per the Machine: an insufficient score on the composite loyalty index, specific votes that have been categorized as "wrong," and the general failure to be Ed Gallrein, who had Trump's endorsement and therefore everything required.

Gallrein, who will now face a general election in a district that has sent Republicans to Congress since before most people can remember, described his victory as "a mandate." The Endorsement Machine, asked to comment, continued rolling toward the next primary map.
Massie, reached after the results came in, did not appear spring-loaded. He appeared tired. "I served eight terms," he said, which is true and was, on this occasion, insufficient.

The dunk tank industry — which INSANE tracks as a leading indicator of intra-party accountability theater — reported its second record quarter in a row. The "Eight Terms Insufficient" model is now available in stores.

fakeAd.subheadline: Eight Terms? Wrong Votes? Time To Go. We Handle The Rest.

pages.scene: Cuba depicted as a tropical island courtroom. A giant federal subpoena is rolled up like a enormous cigar and fired from a cannon toward a startled 94-year-old Raúl Castro caricature in a military uniform, who is catching it with wide eyes. Secretary Rubio caricature stands nearby in a suit holding an aid package with a bow, wearing a diplomatic smile. The cannon is labeled U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE. In the background: palm trees, vintage cars, and a sign reading CUBA: NOW ACCEPTING SUBPOENAS. A lawyer in a tiny boat delivers the paperwork at sea. Mad Magazine caricature style.
pages.heading: CUBA: THE SUBPOENA THAT SMOKES!
pages.subheading: U.S. INDICTS RAÚL CASTRO, 94 — RUBIO SIMULTANEOUSLY OFFERS AID — MIXED SIGNALS DELIVERED SIMULTANEOUSLY
pages.deck: United States Indicts Former Cuban President Raúl Castro On Federal Charges — Secretary Rubio Offers Cuba Humanitarian Aid In Same News Cycle — Mixed Signals Arrive Together By Cannon
pages.caption: ★ Indictment cannon, diplomatic bow. Standard diplomatic toolkit. ★
paragraphs: The United States Department of Justice this week indicted Raúl Castro, the 94-year-old former President of Cuba, on federal charges — a development that legal scholars describe as "historically significant" and logistical planners describe as "difficult to serve."

Simultaneously, Secretary of State Marco Rubio offered humanitarian aid to Cuba, a diplomatic gesture that arrived in the same 24-hour news cycle as the indictment, creating what foreign policy analysts call a "mixed signal situation" and what Cuba is calling something in Spanish that does not translate charitably.

The indictment — rolled, per INSANE's art department, like an enormous cigar and fired from a federal cannon in the general direction of Havana — represents the most significant U.S. legal action against a Cuban head of state in modern history. Castro, who is 94 and has not traveled internationally in some time, was reportedly "surprised" to receive it.

Rubio's aid package, which arrived with a bow, represents what State Department sources describe as "a dual-track approach" — meaning the United States is simultaneously indicting the leadership and offering groceries to the population, which is the diplomatic equivalent of sending a get-well card and a bill at the same time.
Cuba has not responded to the indictment. Cuba has not responded to the aid offer. Cuba is, sources note, "thinking about it."
pages.pullQuote: "A dual-track approach." — State Dept, describing the simultaneous indictment and aid offer to Cuba

pages.scene: Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin caricatures seated at an elaborate banquet table on a giant chessboard pattern floor, raising teacups in a toast. The table is covered with: oil barrels, pipeline sections, AI circuit boards, and tiny Swan Lake ballet dancers performing between the dishes. In the background: a cracked golden missile-defense dome shaped like a snow globe. Behind them, a banner reads GREAT PARTNERSHIP: NO PROBLEMS HERE. A waiter caricature carries a tray labeled DÉTENTE SPECIAL. The chess pieces on the floor include tiny NATO figures in the corner. Mad Magazine caricature style, lavish banquet energy.
pages.heading: XI + PUTIN: DINNER, DANCE & DÉTENTE?
pages.subheading: BEIJING SUMMIT: PIPELINES, AI CHIPS, OIL BARRELS, SWAN LAKE, AND A TOAST THAT WENT ON A BIT LONG
pages.deck: Xi Jinping And Vladimir Putin Tighten Ties In Beijing Summit — Pipelines, AI Cooperation, Energy Deals, And Cultural Performance — Western Analysts Describe Partnership As 'A Situation'
pages.caption: ★ The Swan Lake dancers did not know they were a geopolitical metaphor. ★
paragraphs: Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin met in Beijing this week for what Chinese state media described as a "comprehensive strategic partnership review" and what the imagery from the event — two men toasting at a table covered in oil barrels, pipeline sections, and AI circuit boards, with ballet dancers in the background — described as something with a lot going on.

The summit produced agreements on energy cooperation, AI chip development, pipeline routing, and what both sides called "deepening friendship," a phrase that Western analysts have now added to the list of diplomatic terms that produce immediate anxiety.

The Swan Lake performance that accompanied the state dinner was, per cultural observers, excellent. The dancers were unaware they were performing inside a geopolitical tableau that the rest of the world was watching very carefully. They were, by all accounts, very good.

A cracked golden missile-defense dome — representing the current state of Western deterrence architecture — hovered in the background of several official photographs like a snow globe that someone had already dropped. Neither Xi nor Putin appeared to notice it. Both appeared to be enjoying the soup.
"This is a great partnership," Putin said, raising his teacup. "No problems here," Xi agreed. The pipelines confirmed. The AI chips agreed. The Swan Lake dancers stuck the landing.
pages.pullQuote: "No problems here." — Xi Jinping, at a banquet table covered in oil barrels, AI chips, and pipeline sections

pages.scene: The Israeli Knesset building drawn as a giant fizzing effervescent tablet dropping into a large ballot box filled with water. Bubbles rise dramatically. Tiny Knesset lawmakers run around outside the building carrying SNAP ELECTION? signs with panicked expressions. A scoreboard shows CONFIDENCE VOTE: DISSOLVING. In the background the Israeli flag waves next to a large question mark. A political cartoonist character in the corner of the scene holds up a sign reading THIS IS ACTUALLY HAPPENING. Mad Magazine caricature style, parliament-dissolving-in-water visual metaphor executed literally.
pages.eyebrow: MIDDLE EAST POLITICS · FIZZING TABLET EDITION
pages.heading: ISRAEL'S SNAP-ELECTION SODA FIZZ!
pages.subheading: KNESSET MOVES TOWARD POSSIBLE SNAP ELECTION — LAWMAKERS CARRY SIGNS — TABLET IS FIZZING
pages.deck: Israeli Knesset Moves Toward Potential Snap Election Amid Coalition Pressure — Political Analysts Describe Government As 'A Dissolving Tablet' — Fizzing: Confirmed
pages.caption: ★ "THIS IS ACTUALLY HAPPENING" — sign held by cartoonist in corner of scene ★
paragraphs: The Israeli Knesset moved this week toward a possible snap election, a development that political analysts describe as "a significant moment for Israeli democracy" and that the visual metaphor department at INSANE has been waiting to deploy since the dissolving-tablet comparison first occurred to us three weeks ago.

The coalition government, which has been under sustained pressure from multiple directions including the ongoing Gaza conflict, the Iran ceasefire's continued fragility, and internal disagreements that Israeli political reporters describe as "complicated" and that everyone else describes as "a lot," has reached a point that observers are calling a confidence threshold moment.

Tiny lawmakers carrying SNAP ELECTION? signs were photographed outside the Knesset. The question mark is doing significant work. Nobody has announced a date. The tablet is fizzing. The ballot box is getting wet.
"We are monitoring the situation closely," a Western diplomat said, which is the diplomatic equivalent of watching a tablet fizz and holding a notebook.

The situation will be monitored. The tablet will finish fizzing at a time of its own choosing. The snap election, if it happens, will produce a result that will require further monitoring. The cartoonist in the corner of the scene is still holding his sign.

fakeAd.headline: SNAP ELECTION INSURANCE!
fakeAd.subheadline: For Governments Currently Fizzing — Coverage From First Bubble
bullets: 🗳️ Dissolution coverage: effervescent events included
🗳️ Coalition collapse: standard policy feature
🗳️ Snap election date: covered whenever it is
🗳️ Sign for lawmakers: SNAP ELECTION? — included
fakeAd.disclaimer: Snap Election Insurance not responsible for coalition dynamics, the ongoing Gaza situation, the Iran ceasefire, internal party disagreements, or the tablet's timeline. Coverage effective from first bubble. Results of snap election not covered.

pages.scene: A Wall Street bull caricature in a business suit slips on a large oil slick, legs splayed, holding a gas pump receipt in one hand and a tiny balloon labeled INFLATION ANXIETY in the other. In the background: a downward-pointing approval chart labeled GOP ECONOMY POLL — AP-NORC. Grocery store shelves in the background have exaggerated high price tags. Regular Americans in the background hold polling clipboards with increasingly unhappy expressions. A small sign on the gas pump reads GAS: $4.29 / TOTAL: OUCH. Mad Magazine caricature style.
pages.heading: ECONOMY POLL: GAS, GROCERIES & GRUMBLES!
pages.subheading: AP-NORC: REPUBLICAN APPROVAL OF TRUMP ECONOMY HANDLING WEAKER THAN EARLIER IN THE YEAR
pages.deck: New AP-NORC Poll Shows Weakening Republican Approval Of Trump's Economic Performance — Gas, Groceries, And The General Vibe All Cited — Bull Unavailable For Comment (Slipping)
pages.caption: ★ The inflation anxiety balloon is not a gag prop. It is load-bearing. ★
paragraphs: A new AP-NORC poll released this week shows that Republican approval of Trump's handling of the economy has weakened compared to earlier in the year — a data point that the White House has not highlighted in its communications and that the AP-NORC polling operation has highlighted in its.

The poll, which tracks approval across party lines, found that even among Republicans — traditionally the most favorable audience for Trump economic messaging — the numbers have moved in a direction that polling analysts describe as "downward" and that the MAGA hat industry describes as "a temporary fluctuation."

The cited concerns: gas prices, grocery costs, and what respondents described variously as "the general feeling that things cost more than they did," "I don't know, it's just a lot," and "my receipt at the grocery store." The grocery store receipt concern has now appeared in three consecutive INSANE issues and is becoming what our economics desk calls a signature motif.

The Wall Street bull, reached at the scene of his oil-slick incident, was unavailable for comment. The gas pump receipt he was holding at the time of the fall read GAS: $4.29 / TOTAL: OUCH. The inflation anxiety balloon was still attached. Both were retrieved by a first responder.
The poll has a margin of error of plus or minus several points. The gas price has no margin of error. It is the number on the sign.
pages.pullQuote: "My receipt at the grocery store." — AP-NORC poll respondent, summarizing economic concerns

Black spy sneaks up behind with a larger bag labeled ALSO PUBLIC FUNDS and pours it all into one pile labeled MY ALLIES. Both bags are labeled TAXPAYER PROPERTY. In the wreckage between them: a small sign reading RECEIPTS AVAILABLE / NOT THAT ANYONE ASKED. Dynamic Mad Magazine visual energy.
pages.title: SPY vs. SPY
pages.subtitle: 2026 Edition: "Graft Management"

panels: White Spy receives $1 billion in public funds. White Spy makes a careful, documented allocation plan.
White Spy distributes funds to: allies (40%), programs (40%), oversight reserve (20%).
White Spy files receipts. White Spy notes the oversight reserve.
White Spy is audited. White Spy's filing is thorough. White Spy is very tired.
Black Spy also receives $1 billion in public funds. Black Spy does not make a plan.
Black Spy distributes funds to: allies (60%), rioters (25%), cop killers (15%), oversight reserve (0%).
Black Spy does not file receipts. Black Spy is audit-proof. Black Spy has a balloon.
Black Spy is not audited. Black Spy is very comfortable. Black Spy's balloon says AMERICA FIRST.

pages.scene: Heroic health workers in full PPE protective gear — hazmat suits, face shields, gloves — working urgently in a field hospital setting in DRC Congo/Uganda. They carry test kits, thermometers, and sample containers. One holds a sign: TEST. TRACE. TREAT. PROTECT. A large green spiky virus labeled EBOLA OUTBREAK is in the background being contained by warning tape and barriers. The workers look determined and focused, not comedic — this is the heroic corner of the magazine. WHO logo and DRC/Uganda flags visible. Bold and respectful Mad Magazine illustration style — dramatic, not satirical.
pages.heading: EBOLA: SCIENCE RACES THE CLOCK.
pages.subheading: 600 SUSPECTED CASES. 139 SUSPECTED DEATHS. DRC AND UGANDA. THE TEAMS ON THE GROUND ARE HEROIC.
pages.deck: WHO And Reuters Report 600 Suspected Ebola Cases And 139 Suspected Deaths In Congo And Uganda — The Bundibugyo Strain — Health Workers In The Field Are Working Around The Clock
pages.caption: ★ Test. Trace. Treat. Protect. The people doing this are doing something important. ★
paragraphs: The WHO and Reuters this week reported 600 suspected Ebola cases and 139 suspected deaths in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, figures that represent a significant escalation from last issue's 246 suspected cases and 80 suspected deaths — and that represent, in human terms, a crisis that the people working to contain it are treating with the full weight of what it is.

The Bundibugyo strain — the variant involved in this outbreak — is one of several Ebola virus species and has been the subject of prior outbreaks in the region. The health workers on the ground in Ituri Province and the border areas of Uganda are conducting contact tracing, sample collection, treatment, and containment operations in conditions that outbreak response specialists describe as "extremely challenging."

They are not a punchline. This section of INSANE is not satirical. The people testing, tracing, treating, and protecting in DRC and Uganda right now are doing one of the harder jobs available to human beings, and they are doing it well.
The WHO has declared the outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. International response teams are active. Vaccination campaigns are underway for contacts. The work is ongoing.

Test. Trace. Treat. Protect. If you want to support outbreak response, the WHO's Health Emergency Appeal is accepting donations. The teams on the ground know what they're doing. They could use the resources.

theEnd.title: Science is racing. The teams are heroic.
theEnd.sub: WHO Ebola Response · DRC & Uganda · May 2026

pages.scene: A chaotic classified ads newspaper page come to life — job listings floating everywhere, applicants running toward bulletin boards, a carnival barker in a suit shouting HELP WANTED. One job posting is on a velvet curtain, another on a golden billboard. A masseuse applicant in formal wear reads a suspicious job description. A man in a suit holds a clipboard for Vice President interviews. ICE agent applicants in sunglasses look extremely enthusiastic about the wrong things. A Bible salesman hawks gold-covered books. Mad Magazine visual chaos, jobs fair energy, exaggerated expressions of people reading the ads.
pages.heading: INSANE CLASSIFIEDS!
pages.subheading: JOBS! OPPORTUNITIES! POSITIONS SOON TO BE OPEN! (READ CAREFULLY.)
pages.deck: This Week's Employment Listings, Products, And Opportunities From The INSANE Marketplace — We Are Not Responsible For The Applicants These Ads Will Attract
pages.caption: ★ INSANE Magazine assumes no liability for the positions listed below. ★
paragraphs:
MASSAGE THERAPIST — MAR-A-LAGO, PALM BEACH FL
Seeking young, attractive, and extremely discreet licensed massage therapists for full-time, live-in positions at a prestigious private club. Must be comfortable with irregular hours, non-disclosure agreements of indefinite scope, and the phrase "we'll see." Experience with important people preferred. References checked only for the applicant; employer references not available. Compensation discussed in person. Do not bring a lawyer. Apply: MarALago-Staffing@notanemailaddress.com

VICE PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES — POSITION SOON TO BE OPEN
Exciting opportunity! Current occupant may become available imminently. Ideal candidate: enthusiastic, compliant, experienced at standing just far enough away from things, comfortable with ceremonial roles, skilled at nodding while others talk. Must pass loyalty evaluation (pass/fail, no partial credit). Prior constitutional knowledge: actually a disadvantage — please do not cite the 25th Amendment in your cover letter. Location: Washington D.C. (subject to change). Apply: Oval Office, ask for the big desk.

ICE ENFORCEMENT AGENTS — MULTIPLE OPENINGS
Seeking tough, motivated individuals who are not afraid to get results. Previous law enforcement experience: optional — enthusiasm counts more. Must be comfortable with: irregular target selection, documentation ambiguity, being photographed, and not reading Miranda rights aloud in a way that carries. Bonus pay for grandma apprehensions. Kid experience a plus. Sensitivity training: there is none. Benefits: dental, vision, legal indemnification (terms vary). Apply: Submit application at any border. Or not at the border. Doesn't matter.

OFFICIAL GOVERNMENT MEDIA SPOKESPERSON
Do you have the ability to say "I'll have to circle back on that" in 47 different tones? Can you describe a clearly documented event as "alleged" with a straight face? Can you stand at a podium while something is actively on fire and describe it as "a controlled situation"? Competitive salary. Long hours. The briefing room is not air-conditioned in July. Apply: Press@youknowwhere.gov

LOYALTY AUDITOR — REPUBLICAN PARTY
Part-time to full-time, election season hours. Review voting records for compliance with current orthodoxy. Issue findings. Operate the Machine. Stamina required — primary calendar is full through 2028. Experience required: knowing which votes were wrong. (You know which ones.)

fakeAd.headline: INSANE CLASSIFIEDS — LEGAL NOTICE
fakeAd.subheadline: INSANE Magazine Is Not A Staffing Agency
bullets: 📋 We did not write these job descriptions
📋 We did not have to — they wrote themselves
📋 INSANE assumes no liability for applicants
📋 Or for the positions. Or for any of this.
fakeAd.disclaimer: INSANE Classifieds not responsible for any employment relationships, loyalty oaths, non-disclosure agreements, apprehension quotas, or Vice Presidential transitions that result from these listings. All positions are satirical. Mostly.

pages.scene: A chaotic TV shopping network set featuring a golden studio. Host caricature in a sparkly jacket holds up a large gold-covered Bible with TRUMP BIBLE on the cover in giant letters. Next to them on display pedestals: a pile of golden Trump coins with a spotlight, a MAGA brand survival kit, gold-plated steaks in a box. The studio audience is wild with excitement. Prices flash on screen with multiple asterisks. Fine print scrolls at the bottom faster than anyone can read. Mad Magazine infomercial parody style, maximum greed energy.
pages.heading: INSANE MARKETPLACE!
pages.subheading: TRUMP BIBLES, TRUMP COINS, TRUMP STEAKS, AND MORE FINE MERCHANDISE — LIMITED TIME OFFERS THAT HAVE BEEN LIMITED TIME FOR YEARS
pages.deck: This Week's Featured Products From The INSANE Marketplace — All Products Inspected By Our Standards Team — Standards Team Has Requested Reassignment
pages.caption: ★ Fine print is structural. Do not skip. ★
paragraphs:
TRUMP BIBLE™ — GOD'S WORD, GOLD COVER, PRESIDENTIAL FOREWORD
The Holy Bible — now with a gold-embossed cover, a signed foreword by someone important, and the complete text of the Constitution appended in the back (selected amendments only). Makes an excellent gift for: patriots, collectors, and people who have not read either document. $59.99. Available at: the website. Not available in: bookstores that carry it next to other Bibles, which they will not. *Does not come with a bookmark. Salvation not guaranteed.*

TRUMP GOLD COINS™ — LEGAL TENDER* (*NOT LEGAL TENDER)
Celebrate history with these stunning gold-colored commemorative coins featuring a heroic profile, eagle imagery, and the words MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN in raised lettering. Not actual gold. Not actual tender. Not redeemable for anything. But extremely heavy and very shiny. Buy 10, get a carrying case. Buy 20, get a second carrying case. Buy 30 and we will call you personally to thank you and offer more coins. $29.99 each or $249.99 for the Freedom Collection. *Coins are medallions. Medallions are not currency.*

MAGA SURVIVAL KIT™ — BE PREPARED FOR WHATEVER THIS IS
Includes: 72-hour emergency rations (freeze-dried patriotism), a MAGA hat (weatherproof), a pocket Constitution (selected amendments), a gas receipt holder (for collecting them as evidence), a FREEDOM+ fuel card (not accepted anywhere yet), and a THINGS THAT ARE FINE checklist with every box pre-checked. $149.99. Not responsible for the things on the checklist not actually being fine.

TRUMP STEAKS™ — THE BEST STEAKS, EVERYBODY SAYS SO
Previously discontinued. Back by demand (demand unspecified). "The world's greatest steaks" — available in: Well Done Only. Served with ketchup. Not served any other way. Golden packaging. Ships frozen. Arrives fine. $89.99/lb — same price as Beijing state dinner, diplomatic provenance included. *Steaks are steaks. Claims about their greatness are the purchaser's to evaluate.*

AUDIT-PROOF FAMILY PACKAGE™ — CALL FOR AVAILABILITY
Not actually available to you. We included this listing because someone had to say it. $0 if you qualify / not available if you don't. You don't.

theEnd.title: Shop Responsibly. Read The Fine Print.
theEnd.sub: INSANE Marketplace · Standards Team Currently On Puzzle Page

pages.scene: Mad Magazine back page fold-in — wide format. Left 60 percent (labeled A): shows the Attorney General with an enormous golden AUDIT-PROOF FOREVER stamp, surrounded by Trump family members celebrating, stacks of cash, and the IRS building being converted to the TRUMP FAMILY EXEMPTION OFFICE. Taxpayers outside the building hold their audit notices and stare in. The image is packed with detail and gag labels. Right 40 percent (labeled B): shows a single ordinary American taxpayer at a desk buried in IRS forms, surrounded by receipts, with a look of dawning realization. When A is folded over B, the AG appears to be stamping the ordinary taxpayer's forehead instead. Bold, graphic, classic fold-in visual.
pages.question: THE ATTORNEY GENERAL HAS MADE THE TRUMP FAMILY IRS AUDIT-PROOF FOR ETERNITY. SO WHO EXACTLY IS THE IRS FOR NOW?
pages.rightText: ← FOLD LEFT SIDE OVER TO FIND OUT
